name: Hotel Pitrizza, a Luxury Collection Hotel

content.verdict: If Cala di Volpe is the theatrical older sibling, Pitrizza is the one that writes poetry in private. A cluster of 54 rooms and villas hidden among the granite outcrops of Liscia di Vacca, this is where Costa Smeralda veterans come when they have outgrown the Porto Cervo scene. The seawater pool carved directly into the rocks is the single most photographed pool in Italy — but the real honeymoon magic is the almost monastic calm: no kids' club, no loud buffet, just juniper-scented paths leading from your villa to a small private beach where a butler remembers how you take your morning cappuccino.
content.best_room: The Junior Suite Sea View with private garden is the sweet spot — generous, private, and framed by the granite scenery. For once-in-a-lifetime, the Villa Pitrizza (formerly used by Karim Aga Khan) sits on its own promontory with pool, multiple bedrooms, and full butler service.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and sunset at the rock pool
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Olbia in about 40 minutes. Villa check-in with your dedicated butler, who walks you through the property's hidden paths. Champagne and aperitivi by the seawater rock pool as the sun drops behind Isola delle Bisce.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private beach and long lunch at La Vela
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning on the hotel's small private cove with attendants, fresh fruit, and a paddleboard if you want it. Seafood lunch at La Vela, the beachfront restaurant with toes-in-sand tables.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Boat day to Budelli and Spargi
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge charters a private gozzo or Frauscher for a La Maddalena circuit. Swim at Cala Corsara, see the pink sands of Budelli from offshore, anchor for a long lunch at Passata di Papa on Santa Maria.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pevero Bay hike and spa afternoon
itinerary_7_nights.description: Early morning walk along the Pevero coastal path, then a long couple's treatment at the Pitrizza spa using local myrtle and juniper oils. Dinner at sister property Cala di Volpe's Beefbar — the shuttle takes 8 minutes.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Porto Cervo in low gear
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late-morning stroll around Porto Cervo marina, a coffee at Il Pomodoro, boutique browsing on the Promenade. Back to Pitrizza for a rock-pool afternoon and a sunset dinner on the terrace with Vermentino and spaghetti ai ricci.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Day trip to Palau and La Maddalena town
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 25 minutes to Palau, ferry to La Maddalena for lunch at La Grotta, explore Caprera's Garibaldi house. Quiet villa dinner in-room tonight — the kitchen will set a private table on your terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Lazy last morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late breakfast served on the terrace, one last swim, and the car to Olbia. Many couples extend with two nights in Alghero or an inland Gallura agriturismo to slow the decompression.

caveats: Open roughly mid-May to early October only — outside these months the entire resort closes.
There is no single dramatic restaurant building; dining is intimate and spread across small pavilions, which some guests expecting grander scale find understated.
Access is via stairs and granite paths throughout — mobility-restricted travelers should request a villa close to the main buildings at booking.

faqs.question: How is Pitrizza different from Cala di Volpe?
faqs.answer: Both are Luxury Collection siblings, but Pitrizza is smaller (54 keys vs 121), quieter, more villa-style, and has no kids' club. Cala di Volpe has more restaurants and scene; Pitrizza has more privacy.

faqs.question: Is it truly adults-oriented?
faqs.answer: There is no formal adults-only policy, but the layout, absence of kids' programming, and the pricing all skew toward couples. Most honeymooners find it very peaceful even in August.

faqs.question: Can we use facilities at Cala di Volpe and Romazzino?
faqs.answer: Yes — guests of any of the three sister Luxury Collection properties can dine and use beaches at the others. A free shuttle loops between them all day.

faqs.question: Is breakfast included?
faqs.answer: Breakfast is typically included in the rate, served either at the main restaurant terrace or in-villa. Many honeymooners opt for in-villa breakfast on the private terrace at least twice.

faqs.question: Do we need a car?
faqs.answer: Not essential — the shuttle to Porto Cervo and sister hotels is free. But a car or chauffeur gives you freedom to reach Spiaggia del Principe, Capriccioli, and other quiet coves.

faqs.question: How does the seawater pool work?
faqs.answer: It is filled directly from the sea and refreshed continuously, carved into the natural granite so it feels like a private cove. Heated in shoulder season; unheated in high summer.

content.email_template:
Subject: Honeymoon booking inquiry — Hotel Pitrizza

Buongiorno,

We are planning our honeymoon for [DATES] and would love to stay 6 to 7 nights at Pitrizza. Could you please send best-available rates for a Junior Suite Sea View with private garden, and let us know if any villa upgrades are possible?

Specifically:
1. Which rooms offer the most direct access to the seawater rock pool?
2. Is a butler assigned automatically, or only with villa categories?
3. Can you arrange a private Frauscher or gozzo charter for a day on La Maddalena?
4. Any honeymoon amenities (champagne, in-villa breakfast on arrival, a private dinner on the terrace) you can offer?

Grazie infinite,
[Your names]
